sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Hmm, I clicked on the top purple square (at row 5, col 31) instead of the player purple square. The code found the first purple node which was the one at the top. I need to find the player-specific purple square. Let me check both purple squares and identify which one is the player. The player should be the one that was previously the white cross, at around row 53, col 10.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find all purple squares and identify the player
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p' and n['pixels'] == 9:
        b = n['boundary']
        r = sum(r for r,c in b) // len(b)
        c = sum(c for r,c in b) // len(c)
        print(f"Purple square: id={n['id']} center=({r}, {c}) boundary={b}")

# The player should be near row 53, col 10 (where white cross was)
# The other purple is at row 5, col 31 (top center marker)
# Let me click on the player purple square near row 53
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 53, 'col': 10}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
